name: Baked Squid with Garlic-Anchovy Pasta
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Small squid, cleaned, bodies sliced into 1/4-inch rings, tentacles halved if large
1 cup Plain dried bread crumbs (about 4 ounces)
1/2 lb Dried capellini (or other thin pasta)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Bring a large saucepan of water to a boil.
2. In a large bowl, toss the squid with the bread crumbs, oregano, pepper, and one teaspoon salt. Spread the squid in a single layer in a large baking dish and sprinkle with any leftover crumbs. Drizzle 1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons olive oil on top. Bake for 10 minutes, or until the squid is golden brown and crunchy.
3. Meanwhile, in a small saucepan, whisk the garlic and anchovy paste into the remaining 1/4 cup olive oil and bring to a simmer over low heat. Cook, whisking, until fragrant but not browned, about 3 minutes.
4. Add salt to the boiling water. Add the capellini and cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente, about 3 minutes. Drain the pasta and return it to the saucepan. Add the anchovy sauce and butter and toss to coat.
5. Make a bed of capellini on platters or plates. Mound the baked squid on top and serve with lemon wedges.
